report_name: Open-Source Devs Targeted in Sophisticated Phishing Attack Using Slack and Google Sites

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Attackers sent direct messages on Slack containing a spearphishing link to a malicious Google Sites page to harvest credentials.

attack_patterns.name: Impersonation
attack_patterns.description: Attackers impersonated a Linux Foundation official on Slack to build trust and socially engineer open-source developers.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Web Browsers
attack_patterns.description: A fake Google Workspace login portal hosted on Google Sites was used to harvest developer credentials entered into their web browser.

attack_patterns.name: Install Root Certificate
attack_patterns.description: Developers were tricked into installing a malicious root CA, disguised as a 'security certificate', to enable Man-in-the-Middle attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Adversary-in-the-Middle
attack_patterns.description: After the installation of a malicious root certificate, attackers can perform Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks to intercept and decrypt all of the victim's network traffic.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: The macOS variant of the attack uses scripting, such as 'curl ... | bash', to download and execute additional malicious payloads.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution
attack_patterns.description: The attack relies on the user executing the final stage of the attack by being tricked into installing the malicious root certificate file.

malware.name: Malicious Root Certificate
malware.description: A malicious root CA certificate disguised as a 'security certificate'. Once installed on a developer's machine, it enables attackers to perform Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks to intercept and decrypt all of the victim's network traffic.

campaigns.name: Open-Source Developer Slack Phishing Campaign
campaigns.description: A social engineering campaign targeting open-source developers on Slack, impersonating the Linux Foundation to lure victims to a phishing page on Google Sites, with the goal of stealing credentials and installing a malicious root certificate for MitM attacks.
campaigns.objective: Compromise developer accounts to enable software supply chain attacks.

identities.name: Open-source developers
identities.description: The primary target group for a social engineering campaign aimed at compromising the software supply chain.

identities.name: Linux Foundation
identities.description: A non-profit technology consortium whose officials were impersonated by attackers on Slack to gain the trust of developers.

identities.name: Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF)
identities.description: An open-source community whose members were targeted by the phishing campaign.

identities.name: Open Source Security Foundation (OpenSSF)
identities.description: An organization that issued warnings about the social engineering campaign targeting open-source developers.

infrastructure.name: Phishing Page on Google Sites
infrastructure.description: A malicious page hosted on Google Sites, replicating a Google Workspace login portal to harvest developer credentials and deliver a malicious root certificate.

infrastructure.name: MitM Traffic Interception Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Implied infrastructure used to receive and process network traffic intercepted via the malicious root certificate installed on victim machines.

indicators.name: macOS Malicious Certificate Installation via Security Command
indicators.pattern:
title: macOS Malicious Certificate Installation via Security Command
id: 2b1c8f9a-3d4e-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of the 'security' command-line tool on macOS to add a trusted certificate, a technique used in a campaign targeting open-source developers.
references:
  - https://www.techzine.eu/2026/04/14/attackers-are-targeting-developers-via-slack-and-google-sites/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-14
tags:
  - attack.t1553.004
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: macos
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/security'
    CommandLine|contains: 'add-trusted-cert'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the `security add-trusted-cert` command on macOS, which was used by attackers to install a malicious root certificate.

indicators.name: Windows Malicious Certificate Installation via Certutil
indicators.pattern:
title: Windows Malicious Certificate Installation via Certutil
id: 9d8c7b6a-5f4e-4d3c-b2a1-0987654321fe
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'certutil.exe' to install a new root certificate into the trusted store, a technique used to enable Man-in-the-Middle attacks.
references:
  - https://www.techzine.eu/2026/04/14/attackers-are-targeting-developers-via-slack-and-google-sites/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-14
tags:
  - attack.t1553.004
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\certutil.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '-addstore'
      - 'root'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of `certutil -addstore root` on Windows to install a malicious root certificate, as mentioned in the article's cyber observables.

indicators.name: Suspicious Script Execution via Curl Pipe to Bash
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Script Execution via Curl Pipe to Bash
id: 5a4b3c2d-1e9f-4a8b-9c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects a common infection vector on Linux/macOS where a script is downloaded via curl and piped directly into a shell for execution.
references:
  - https://www.techzine.eu/2026/04/14/attackers-are-targeting-developers-via-slack-and-google-sites/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-14
tags:
  - attack.t1059
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection_curl:
    Image|endswith: '/curl'
  selection_bash:
    ParentImage|endswith: '/bash'
    Image|endswith: '/curl' # Bash is the parent of curl in a pipe
  filter_pipe:
    CommandLine|contains: '|'
  condition: (1 of selection_*) and filter_pipe
indicators.description: Detects the `curl ... | bash` command pattern, a common method for downloading and executing malicious scripts on Linux and macOS systems, as noted in the article.

relationships.source_name: macOS Malicious Certificate Installation via Security Command
Windows Malicious Certificate Installation via Certutil
Suspicious Script Execution via Curl Pipe to Bash
